core_mechanism: data-parallel decomposition of an all-pairs (N-body) interaction/force summation onto a massively parallel SIMD many-thread architecture, mapping each interacting element to an independent thread that accumulates its interaction with the others (with separate handling for short-range and long-range coupling)
brief_reason: The abstract's method is porting the pairwise short-ranged and long-ranged interaction computations of an N-body dynamics simulation onto the many-core SIMD hardware of a GPU, achieving large speedups by evaluating the interaction sum concurrently across processing threads.
